label: May 24, 2026
headline: Trump says agreement with Iran 'largely negotiated,' includes opening strait
summary: Former President Donald Trump announced a "largely negotiated" agreement with Iran, pending finalisation. The proposal reportedly includes opening the crucial Strait of Hormuz, a key international shipping lane. This development followed discussions with leaders from the UAE and Saudi Arabia.
gd_angle: Strategic geography, energy security, international diplomacy, impact on global trade routes, US-Middle East relations.

label: May 24, 2026
headline: CM reviews projects to curb air pollution
summary: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta reviewed three pilot projects aimed at year-round air pollution control. These initiatives include 21 filter-free air purifying systems, an electric vehicle-based anti-smog gun, and a roadside air pollution control device. The focus is on deploying strategic technology to improve urban air quality.
gd_angle: Environment policy, urban sustainability, strategic technology for public health, governance in environmental management.

label: May 24, 2026
headline: Director of Public Health inspects Ebola screening facility at Chennai airport
summary: Following a Union Health Ministry advisory on Ebola, the Director of Public Health in Tamil Nadu inspected screening facilities at Chennai airport. This proactive measure ensures preparedness against the Ebola virus disease. The focus is on implementing health protocols for international travellers to safeguard national health security.
gd_angle: National health security, disaster preparedness, strategic public health infrastructure, international health cooperation.

label: May 23, 2026
headline: Rajasthan Cabinet approves new industrial development policy with focus on talent economy
summary: Rajasthan's Cabinet approved a new industrial development policy aiming for a $350 billion State economy by 2028-29. The policy prioritizes the talent economy and private sector participation to establish world-class industrial infrastructure. This move reflects a strategic focus on economic growth and job creation within the state.
gd_angle: Economic policy, state-led industrial growth, role of talent and private sector in national development.

label: May 23, 2026
headline: Public hearing for limestone mining called off in Meghalaya after protest
summary: A public hearing for a ₹1,800-crore integrated cement plant and limestone mining project in Meghalaya's East Jaiñtia Hills was cancelled due to protests and a brief stand-off between groups. This highlights local opposition to large-scale industrial projects and the challenges of balancing development with environmental concerns and community interests.
gd_angle: Environmental governance, balancing development with ecological concerns, public participation in industrial projects.

label: May 23, 2026
headline: Bengal CM on first visit to Delhi after taking charge, meets President, Prime Minister and Home Minister
summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari visited Delhi for the first time since taking office, meeting President Droupadi Murmu, Vice President, Prime Minister Narendra Modi, and Home Minister Amit Shah. This series of high-level meetings signifies engagement between the state and central leadership and aims to address previous diplomatic issues.
gd_angle: Centre-state relations, cooperative federalism, importance of dialogue between political leadership for national cohesion.

label: May 23, 2026
headline: Pakistan military chief Asim Munir arrives in Tehran in push to end Iran war
summary: Pakistan's Army Chief Syed Asim Munir arrived in Tehran for mediation efforts between Iran and the United States, meeting Iran's Interior Minister. This visit follows a Qatari delegation's talks with the Iranian Foreign Minister, signaling regional diplomatic initiatives to de-escalate tensions and potentially end the ongoing conflict.
gd_angle: Regional security dynamics, diplomatic efforts to de-escalate conflicts, implications of Middle Eastern geopolitics for global stability.

This highlights the critical role of robust infrastructure in fostering national economic development and attracting investment.

label: May 23, 2026
headline: HMT revival package at final stage, says Union Minister

The package was initially announced in August 2024, signaling government support for the public sector undertaking.

label: May 22, 2026
headline: West Asia crisis hurting Indian tea exporters
summary: The ongoing crisis in West Asia is negatively affecting Indian tea exporters, particularly from North Indian regions like Assam and West Bengal. India exported a significant 280.4 million kg of tea in 2025, valued at ₹8,488 crore, with a substantial portion destined for West Asia. This geopolitical instability poses a challenge to India's agricultural exports and economic stability.
gd_angle: Geopolitical events, economic impact, supply chain resilience, India's export diversification strategy;

The incident underscores ongoing tensions regarding collective security responses to global threats.

label: May 22, 2026
headline: Supreme Court declines to interfere with forest clearances for Adani’s Madhya Pradesh coal block project
summary: The Supreme Court has upheld the environmental clearances for the Adani Group's coal block project in Madhya Pradesh's Singrauli district. The court cited delays in challenging the approvals, dismissing a plea by an environmental activist. This decision balances industrial development and environmental concerns, emphasizing timely legal challenges in such matters.
gd_angle: Sustainable development, environment vs. economy debate, judicial activism, India's energy policy;

label: May 22, 2026
headline: Sedition trials can go on if accused is willing: Supreme Court
summary: The Supreme Court has allowed sedition trials under Section 124A IPC to proceed if the accused consents, four years after pausing such trials. The previous hold was to permit the government to re-examine the colonial-era provision. This ruling navigates the balance between national security and individual liberties, signaling potential judicial re-evaluation while awaiting legislative action.
gd_angle: Freedom of speech, national security, constitutional validity of sedition law, judicial interpretation of archaic laws;

This potential visit highlights China's growing influence in South Asia and Bangladesh's foreign policy alignments. Such engagements are significant for regional geopolitical dynamics and India's neighbourhood strategy.
gd_angle: Neighbourhood diplomacy, China's growing influence, regional power dynamics, India's strategic response in South Asia;

gd_angle: Internal security challenges, strategies for counter-insurgency and Naxalism, the role of security forces in maintaining peace, national development in conflict-affected areas, and governance reforms.

label: May 20, 2026
headline: Any step towards closing Tasmac shops is only welcome: HC
summary: The Madurai Bench of the Madras High Court on Wednesday while disposing of petitions filed by land owners and persons holding licence to sell snacks at Tasmac shops seeking a direction to forbear the authorities from closing the shops, said the court will not consider the doctrine of legitimate expectation as in ordinary commerce since the business itself is considered to be harmful to public health and public welfare. The court said Article 47 of the Constitution under the Directive Principles of State Policy states the State shall endeavour to bring about prohibition of the consumption except for medicinal purposes of intoxicating drinks and of drugs which are injurious to health.
gd_angle: Constitutional values, public welfare, state policy.

label: May 20, 2026
headline: AIKKMS urges Centre to withdraw VB-G RAM-G Act, continue earlier scheme
summary: Members of the All India Kisan Khet Mazdoor Sanghatan (AIKKMS) on Tuesday submitted a memorandum to the President and the Chief Minister through the Chief Executive Officer of the Kalaburagi Zilla Panchayat demanding withdrawal of the proposed Viksit Bharat Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevik Mission (Gramin) (VB-G RAM-G) Act 2025 and continuation of the erstwhile Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA). In the memorandum, the organisation has urged the governments to enhance guaranteed employment under the NREGA from 100 human workdays to 200 human workdays a year and ensure a daily wage of ₹600 for workers.
gd_angle: Socio-economic policy, rural development, public welfare.

label: May 20, 2026
headline: NAPM demands moratorium on punitive enforcement during heat wave
summary: The National Alliance of People’s Movements strongly demanded an immediate moratorium on all eviction drives, livelihood confiscations and punitive enforcement actions against informal workers during peak summer months and heat wave periods.
gd_angle: Climate resilience, social equity, worker protection.

gd_angle: Fiscal policy, economic governance, financial literacy.

label: May 20, 2026
headline: KTR praises Foxconn facility, says MoU inked during BRS regime yielding thousands of jobs
summary: While $500 million (around ₹4,100 crore) was initial investment committed, when the project broke ground in May 2023, it since has been raised to ₹5,000 crore. Foxconn had increased its target investment to $550 million (around ₹4,800 crore). Over ₹3,000 crore has been deployed on the facility, which includes the 1.4 million sq ft factory at Kongara Kalan, Telangana. The MoU inked during the BRS regime is yielding thousands of jobs.
gd_angle: Economic growth, foreign investment, strategic manufacturing.

headline: Rules for new rural employment scheme to be issued soon
summary: Rules governing the new rural employment guarantee scheme, the Viksit Bharat – Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin), which is scheduled to come into force from July 1, will be released by the government in the next few days, officials of the Union Ministry of Rural Development informed a Parliamentary Standing Committee on Wednesday.

label: May 20, 2026
headline: Telangana gets ₹1,420 cr. central aid for projects in Warangal, Karimnagar and Khammam
summary: The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs’ (MoHUA) first National Apex Committee (NAC) for the Urban Challenge Fund (UCF) has, in principle, approved three projects across 26 urban local bodies in Telangana with a total outlay of ₹5,681.6 crore, with central aid amounting to ₹1,420 crore. Projects include a ₹5,681.6 crore underground sewerage network in Warangal and a ₹840 crore project in Karimnagar covering solid waste management, riverfront development, stormwater drains, roads, and a skywalk.
gd_angle: Urban development, infrastructure funding, sustainable cities.
